name: Quick Rising Oat Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously grease two 8x4 or 9x5-inch loaf pans.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine whole wheat flour, oats, yeast, and salt.
3. Heat 1-3/4 cups water, syrups, and margarine until very warm (120 to 130 degrees F).
4. Pour the heated liquid over the oats mixture; add 2 eggs.
5. Beat at low speed of an electric mixer for 1 minute.
6. Continue beating at medium speed for 2 minutes.
7. Gradually stir in enough all-purpose flour to make a soft dough.
8. On a lightly floured surface, knead in enough remaining flour until the dough becomes smooth and elastic; about 10 minutes.
9. Place the dough in a large greased bowl. Cover; let rise in a warm place for about 45 minutes or until doubled in size.
10. Punch down the dough. Divide into 2 parts; shape each into a ball.
11. Allow the dough to rest, covered with an inverted bowl, for about 15 minutes.
12. Shape each ball into a loaf. Place in prepared pans. Cover; let rise in a warm place for about 1/2 hour or until doubled in size.
13. Preheat oven to 375 F.
14. Combine remaining egg and 1 tablespoon water; brush over loaves.
15. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until the loaves sound hollow when tapped lightly.
16. Remove loaves from pans immediately. Cool on a wire rack.
2 LOAVES (18 SLICES EACH)
NUTRITIONAL ANALYSIS per slice:
calories 111
carbohydrates 18 g
protein 3 g
fat 3 g
calcium 10 mg
sodium 160 mg
cholesterol 25 mg
dietary fiber 1 g
